applied_optimizations.name: Triton fused min+tanh+tanh kernel
applied_optimizations.description: Correct Triton kernel implementation with manual tanh (tl.math.exp), vectorized channel loading (tl.arange+tl.load), and hardware reduction (tl.min). Fixed original tl.libdevice.tanh error.

attempted_but_failed.name: torch.compile on ModelNew
attempted_but_failed.reason: Produces incorrect output when combined with custom Triton kernel

notes: Root cause: 8.4M kernel launches creates prohibitive overhead
Triton kernel for min+tanh+tanh: ~52ms vs PyTorch native: ~5ms (10x slower)
This problem pattern (fine-grained reduction+elementwise) is not amenable to Triton speedup on ROCm
Native PyTorch/ROCm kernels are highly optimized for this workload

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has produced no output for 3 consecutive trials (2, 3, 4). The core issue is the Triton kernel processes one spatial position per program (8.4M launches). Need to give an explicit, complete code

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Agent has produced no output for 4 consecutive trials (2-5). Providing complete, copy-paste code and exact commands. The key fix is processing BLOCK_SPATIAL=1024 positions per program using tl.arange

global_notes: KernelBench L2-P25 is challenging for Triton: conv2d dominates (85%), and native ROCm ops for min+tanh are already optimal
